official_basis: Google states that PageRank was among the core ranking systems used when Google first launched and that it has evolved over time.
dominant_visibility_model: Crawlable pages, keyword relevance, links, and early local citations.
old_roofing_tactic: Build pages around exact service keywords and pursue broad link/citation visibility.
today_roofing_implication: Links still matter as one signal, but they need to sit inside a broader proof system with helpful content, local profile accuracy, reviews, structure, and entity consistency.

official_basis: Google describes Panda as a system announced in 2011 to help ensure high-quality and original content appeared in results; it became part of core ranking systems in 2015.
dominant_visibility_model: Content quality and originality became harder to separate from rankings.
old_roofing_tactic: Publish more city and service pages to cover more keyword permutations.
today_roofing_implication: Thin pages, duplicated city copy, and generic best/top pages are liabilities. Pages need useful local evidence and original explanation.

official_basis: Google describes Penguin as a system announced in 2012 to combat link spam; it was integrated into core ranking systems in 2016.
dominant_visibility_model: Link quality and spam resistance became more central.
old_roofing_tactic: Use aggressive anchor text, directory submissions, or low-quality link schemes.
today_roofing_implication: Use legitimate mentions, profiles, press, citations, and useful source material rather than manipulative link acquisition.

official_basis: Google describes Hummingbird as a major improvement to overall ranking systems made in August 2013.
dominant_visibility_model: Search moved further from exact keyword matching toward meaning, entities, and intent.
old_roofing_tactic: Make separate pages for every keyword wording variation.
today_roofing_implication: Group related query variants into hubs and proof modules. Use natural language, FAQs, and intent-based architecture.

public_milestone: RankBrain, neural matching, BERT, and language understanding
official_basis: Google's ranking systems guide describes AI systems including BERT and neural matching; Google's 2019 BERT announcement described better understanding of conversational queries and intent.
dominant_visibility_model: Google became better at matching concepts, intent, passages, and natural-language query meaning.
old_roofing_tactic: Rely on keyword density and exact-match page titles.
today_roofing_implication: Answer real homeowner questions: leak urgency, repair versus replacement, storm documentation, trust criteria, and service-area truth.

official_basis: Google says its automated ranking systems prioritize helpful, reliable information created to benefit people, not content created to manipulate rankings. Google also notes the helpful content system became part of core ranking systems in March 2024.
dominant_visibility_model: Original analysis, clear sourcing, expertise, useful page experience, and people-first content.
old_roofing_tactic: Scale generic SEO copy for more traffic.
today_roofing_implication: Publish original roofing process content, field documentation standards, local proof, author/company background, and evidence-backed trust pages.

public_milestone: Generative AI Search, AI Overviews, AI Mode, RAG, and query fan-out
official_basis: Google says generative AI features are rooted in core Search ranking and quality systems and use retrieval-augmented generation and query fan-out.
dominant_visibility_model: Search may synthesize sources into answers rather than simply listing pages.
old_roofing_tactic: Treat rankings and blue-link clicks as the whole scoreboard.
today_roofing_implication: Make the business easy to cite, summarize, verify, and connect across pages, profiles, datasets, structured data, reviews, and real local proof.

public_milestone: Google Business Profile relevance, distance, and prominence
official_basis: Google Business Profile guidance says local results are mainly based on relevance, distance, and prominence/popularity; complete business information, reviews, replies, photos, and videos matter.
dominant_visibility_model: Local visibility depends on business identity, proximity, profile completeness, reviews, and prominence.
old_roofing_tactic: Treat the map pack as separate from organic strategy.
today_roofing_implication: Unify GBP, website, city pages, reviews, photos, structured data, and published research so local facts agree across surfaces.
